title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor has failed or is sending incorrect signals to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code 1E indicates a malfunction in the water level pressure sensor (also called the pressure switch or frequency sensor). This sensor monitors how much water is in the drum during wash cycles. When the control board cannot read the sensor signal or receives out-of-range readings, it halts the cycle and displays 1E. This issue can stem from a faulty sensor, damaged wiring, a kinked air hose, or a failed main control board.

causes.cause: Kinked or disconnected air hose from tub to sensor
Damaged wiring harness between sensor and control board
Debris or water in the pressure sensor air hose

symptoms: Error code 1E or E7 displayed on panel
Drum may overfill or underfill with water
Washer will not start a new cycle
Buzzing or clicking sounds from the control area

fixSteps.title: Power reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer from the wall outlet for 5 minutes, then plug it back in and attempt a new cycle. This can clear transient sensor errors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the air hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Locate the small rubber hose running from the bottom of the tub to the pressure sensor (usually mounted on the side or rear of the cabinet). Check for kinks, cracks, or disconnections. Straighten any kinks and reconnect if loose.

fixSteps.title: Blow out the air hose
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully disconnect the air hose from the pressure sensor. Gently blow through the hose to clear any debris or water. You should hear a click from the sensor end when air pressure is applied.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test the pressure sensor for continuity. The sensor typically has 3 terminals. Refer to your model's wiring diagram for expected resistance values, usually 3-5 kOhms. Replace the sensor if readings are out of range.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring harness
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring harness from the pressure sensor to the main control board for damage, corrosion, or loose connectors. Repair or replace damaged wires.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the error persists after replacing the pressure sensor and checking all wiring, as the main control board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 1E error code fix

faq.q: What does Samsung washer error 1E mean?
faq.a: Error 1E means the water level pressure sensor is not communicating properly with the main control board. The washer cannot determine how much water is in the drum.

faq.q: Can I still use my washer with a 1E error?
faq.a: No, the washer will not operate correctly with this error. It may overfill or underfill, potentially causing water damage or poor wash results.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix a 1E error?
faq.a: If the pressure sensor is the issue, a DIY repair costs $15-40 for the part. Professional repair typically runs $120-250 including labor.

title: Motor Error
shortDescription: The washer's drive motor is not functioning properly or the control board cannot detect motor rotation.
fullDescription: Error code 3E signals a problem with the washer's drive motor or motor control system. The main control board is unable to detect proper motor operation, which may be caused by a motor failure, a faulty Hall sensor, loose wiring connections, or an overloaded drum. This is a general motor error; Samsung uses sub-codes 3E1 through 3E4 for more specific motor-related failures.

causes.cause: Loose or damaged motor wiring harness
Failed inverter board or main control board

symptoms: Error code 3E or EA on display
Drum does not spin or agitate
Motor hums but drum does not move

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer and remove approximately half of the laundry. Overloading is the most common cause of motor errors. Restart the cycle with a smaller load.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then plug it back in. Press and hold the Start/Pause button for 5 seconds to reset. Start a new cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the motor at the bottom rear of the washer. Check that the motor wire harness connector is firmly seated. Look for burnt or corroded pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the Hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The Hall sensor is a small component mounted on the motor. Using a multimeter, check resistance between the sensor pins (typically 7-15 ohms). If open or shorted, replace the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Test motor windings
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wire harness. Test resistance between each pair of motor winding terminals. Readings should be 2-7 ohms and balanced across all pairs. Significantly different readings indicate a failed motor.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the motor itself is faulty, if the inverter board needs replacement, or if you smell burning from the motor.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 3E error code fix

faq.q: Is a 3E error serious?
faq.a: It can be. While it's sometimes caused by simply overloading the washer, persistent 3E errors may indicate motor or control board failure requiring professional repair.

faq.q: Can overloading cause a 3E error?
faq.a: Yes, overloading is the most common cause. The motor cannot rotate the drum when it's too heavy, triggering the error. Remove some items and retry.

faq.q: What is a Hall sensor?
faq.a: The Hall sensor (tachometer) is a small magnetic sensor on the drive motor that detects motor speed and rotation. It sends RPM data to the control board.

title: Motor Overloaded
shortDescription: The washer motor is overloaded due to excessive laundry or a mechanical obstruction.
fullDescription: Error 3E1 specifically indicates the drive motor is drawing excessive current because the drum load exceeds the motor's capacity. This is distinct from the general 3E code as it specifically points to an overload condition rather than a motor component failure. Common causes include too much laundry, heavy items like comforters or rugs, or a foreign object wedged between the drum and tub.

causes.cause: Washer overloaded with too much laundry
Heavy item like a comforter exceeding motor capacity
Foreign object jammed between inner drum and outer tub
Worn or seized drum bearings increasing friction

symptoms: Cycle stops during agitation or spin

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load size
fixSteps.instruction: Remove about half the laundry from the drum. Samsung front-loaders should not be filled more than 3/4 full. Redistribute the remaining items evenly.

fixSteps.title: Check for foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the drum opening and feel around the rubber door gasket for coins, buttons, or small items that may have fallen between the drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes. Plug back in and run an empty Rinse+Spin cycle to verify the motor operates correctly without a load.

fixSteps.title: Inspect drum bearings
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer empty, spin the drum by hand. It should rotate smoothly with minimal resistance. Grinding, rumbling, or rough rotation indicates worn bearings.

whenToCallPro: If the drum does not spin freely when empty and by hand, the bearings are likely worn and professional replacement is recommended as it requires disassembling the entire tub.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 3E1 error code fix

faq.q: How much laundry is too much for a Samsung washer?
faq.a: Most Samsung front-load washers hold 4.5-5.8 cu ft. Fill the drum loosely to about 3/4 full. You should be able to fit your hand between the top of the laundry and the drum.

faq.q: Can washing a comforter cause 3E1?
faq.a: Yes. Large, heavy items like king-size comforters can exceed the motor's capacity, especially when wet. Use a commercial washer for oversized bedding.

faq.q: Will 3E1 damage my washer?
faq.a: Running the washer repeatedly in an overloaded condition can damage the motor, bearings, and suspension. Always reduce the load when this error appears.

title: Motor Speed Signal Weak
shortDescription: The main control board is receiving a weak or intermittent signal from the motor's speed sensor.
fullDescription: Error 3E2 indicates that the motor's Hall sensor (tachometer) is generating a weak signal. The control board can detect some rotation but the signal is too inconsistent to maintain proper motor speed control. This is often caused by a failing Hall sensor, loose sensor connections, or excessive electrical noise interfering with the signal.

causes.cause: Failing Hall sensor producing weak signal
Loose wiring connection at motor Hall sensor
Corroded connector pins on motor harness

symptoms: Drum spins erratically or at wrong speed
Washer stops during spin cycle intermittently
Spin cycle not reaching full RPM

fixSteps.title: Power cycle and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then restart. Intermittent signal issues can sometimes be resolved with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check Hall sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the motor area. Locate the Hall sensor connector (small plug with thin wires on the motor). Ensure it is firmly connected. Disconnect and reconnect to re-seat the connection.

fixSteps.title: Clean connector pins
fixSteps.instruction: Use electrical contact cleaner to clean the Hall sensor connector pins. Look for corrosion or bent pins. Straighten any bent pins carefully with needle-nose pliers.

fixSteps.title: Replace the Hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the wiring is intact, replace the Hall sensor. It clips onto the motor and connects with a small wire harness. Remove the retaining clip, pull off the old sensor, install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after Hall sensor replacement, the motor or inverter control board may be failing.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 3E2 error code fix

faq.q: What is a Hall sensor on a Samsung washer?
faq.a: The Hall sensor is a small magnetic pickup mounted on the drive motor. It detects how fast the motor is spinning and reports the speed to the control board.

faq.q: Can I replace the Hall sensor myself?
faq.a: Yes, it's a relatively straightforward repair. The sensor clips onto the motor and has a single wire connector. The part costs $8-25.

faq.q: Why does my washer spin at the wrong speed?
faq.a: A weak Hall sensor signal means the control board can't accurately gauge motor speed, causing it to speed up, slow down, or stop unexpectedly.

title: Motor Speed Signal Abnormal
shortDescription: The motor tachometer is generating abnormal signals that do not match expected patterns.
fullDescription: Error 3E3 indicates the Hall sensor signal pattern is abnormal. Unlike 3E2 (weak signal), 3E3 means the signal waveform itself is irregular, suggesting electrical component failure in the sensor or motor. This can also occur if the motor's magnets are damaged or the stator assembly has a defect.

causes.cause: Defective Hall sensor with erratic output
Main control board signal processing failure

symptoms: Motor may start then immediately stop

fixSteps.title: Reset the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes. This clears any stored error states in the control board's memory.

fixSteps.title: Test Hall sensor output
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor. With a multimeter set to AC voltage, check the Hall sensor output while manually spinning the drum. You should see a fluctuating voltage. No reading or erratic spikes indicate a bad sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace Hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old Hall sensor from the motor by unclipping the retaining bracket and disconnecting the wire harness. Install the new sensor and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor stator
fixSteps.instruction: If the Hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve the error, inspect the motor stator for burn marks, cracked magnets, or visible damage. A damaged stator requires motor replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after replacing the Hall sensor, professional diagnosis is needed as the motor or control board likely requires replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 3E3 error code fix

faq.q: Is 3E3 different from 3E?
faq.a: Yes. 3E is a general motor error. 3E3 specifically indicates the motor speed signal waveform is abnormal, pointing more precisely to the Hall sensor or motor stator.

faq.q: Can a bad stator cause 3E3?
faq.a: Yes. Damaged magnets or windings in the stator can produce irregular electrical signals that the Hall sensor picks up, triggering 3E3.

faq.q: How much does a new Samsung washer motor cost?
faq.a: A Samsung washer drive motor assembly typically costs $120-250 for the part. Professional installation adds $100-200 in labor.

title: Motor Stalling or Locked Rotor
shortDescription: The drive motor has stalled and cannot rotate despite receiving power from the control board.
fullDescription: Error 3E4 means the control board is sending power to the motor but detecting zero rotation. The motor is in a locked rotor condition. This can be caused by a physical obstruction preventing drum rotation, seized bearings, a jammed pump, or a failed motor. This error typically occurs suddenly rather than intermittently.

causes.cause: Foreign object jammed in drain pump preventing motor rotation

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Try spinning the drum by hand. If it won't rotate, something is physically blocking it. Check the drain pump filter for foreign objects like coins, buttons, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the access panel at the bottom front of the washer. Place towels down and have a shallow container ready. Open the drain filter cap and remove any debris. Common items include coins, hair ties, and small clothing.

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear of the washer. Inspect the drive belt for breaks, fraying, or if it has come off the pulleys. Replace if damaged. Note: Some Samsung models are direct-drive and have no belt.

fixSteps.title: Test motor for locked rotor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor harness. Try turning the motor shaft by hand. If it won't turn, the motor has seized internally and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the drum won't spin by hand (bearing failure) or if the motor shaft is seized, as these require major disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 3E4 error code fix

faq.q: Why is my Samsung washer drum not spinning at all?
faq.a: A completely motionless drum with 3E4 error is usually caused by a physical obstruction, seized bearings, a broken drive belt, or a failed motor.

faq.q: Can a coin cause a 3E4 error?
faq.a: Yes. Small objects that fall into the drain pump can jam the impeller, which can prevent the motor from turning in some washer configurations.

faq.q: Does my Samsung washer have a drive belt?
faq.a: Many Samsung front-load washers use a direct-drive motor with no belt. Some older models use a belt. Check your model's specifications.

title: Water Supply Error
shortDescription: The washer is not detecting adequate water supply during the fill cycle.
fullDescription: Error code 4E indicates the washer is unable to fill with water within the allotted time. The control board opens the water inlet valves but doesn't detect sufficient water entering the tub. This is one of the most common Samsung washer errors and is usually caused by water supply issues rather than washer component failures. Check your home's water supply first before suspecting washer parts.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets turned off or partially closed
Kinked or frozen water supply hoses

symptoms: Error code 4E, E1, or 4C on display
Washer does not fill with water
Washer starts cycle but stops quickly
No water sound when cycle begins

fixSteps.title: Check water supply faucets
fixSteps.instruction: Verify both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer are fully open. Turn them counterclockwise to the fully open position.

fixSteps.title: Inspect supply hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Check both water supply hoses for kinks, bends, or damage. Straighten any kinks. If the hoses are older than 5 years, consider replacing them with braided stainless steel hoses.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet valve filters
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply faucets. Disconnect the hoses from the back of the washer. Inside each valve inlet, you'll find a small mesh screen filter. Carefully remove them with needle-nose pliers and clean under running water. Reinstall and reconnect hoses.

fixSteps.title: Test water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect a supply hose and hold it over a bucket. Turn on the faucet. Water should flow forcefully. If the flow is weak, you may have a household water pressure issue. Samsung washers require at least 20 psi.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If water supply is confirmed good, test the inlet valve solenoids with a multimeter. Each solenoid should read 500-1500 ohms. Replace the valve assembly if readings are out of range.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if you've verified the water supply is good and cleaned the filters but the washer still won't fill. The inlet valve or control board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 4E error code fix

faq.q: Why is my Samsung washer not filling with water?
faq.a: The most common causes are closed water faucets, kinked supply hoses, or clogged inlet screens. Check these simple items before suspecting a part failure.

faq.q: What does 4C mean on Samsung washer?
faq.a: 4C is the newer version of the 4E error code. Both indicate the same water supply problem. Samsung updated the code display on newer models.

faq.q: How do I clean the water inlet filters?
faq.a: Turn off water, disconnect hoses from the washer, use pliers to carefully pull out the mesh screen from each inlet, rinse under water, and reinstall.

title: Hot and Cold Water Lines Reversed
shortDescription: The hot and cold water supply lines are connected to the wrong inlets on the washer.
fullDescription: Error code 4E1 indicates the washer has detected that the hot and cold water supply hoses are connected to the wrong inlets. Samsung washers with temperature sensors in the inlet valves can detect this reversal. This causes incorrect wash temperatures. During cycles requiring cold water, the washer receives hot water and vice versa, triggering this specific error code.

causes.cause: Hot and cold water hoses swapped at washer connections
Hot and cold water faucets connected to wrong supply lines
Faulty water inlet valve temperature sensor
Hot water heater malfunction sending hot water on cold line

symptoms: Hot water during cold wash cycle
Clothes damaged by unexpected hot water
Steam coming from washer during cold cycle

fixSteps.title: Check hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the back of the washer. The hot water hose (usually red) should connect to the hot inlet (marked H or with red). The cold water hose (usually blue) should connect to the cold inlet (marked C or with blue).

fixSteps.title: Swap the hoses if reversed
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both water faucets. Disconnect both hoses from the washer. Reconnect them to the correct inlets - hot to hot, cold to cold. Tighten by hand plus a quarter turn with pliers.

fixSteps.title: Verify faucet supply lines
fixSteps.instruction: If hoses are correctly connected at the washer, check the wall faucets. Run water from each faucet into a container - verify the hot faucet produces hot water and the cold faucet produces cold water.

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: After correcting the connections, plug the washer back in and run a short warm wash cycle. Verify that water temperature matches the selected setting.

whenToCallPro: If the hoses are correctly connected but the error persists, the inlet valve temperature sensor may be faulty. A technician can diagnose this.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 4E1 error code fix

faq.q: How do I know which water line is hot and which is cold?
faq.a: On most Samsung washers, the hot inlet is on the left (marked H or red) and the cold inlet is on the right (marked C or blue) when viewed from the back.

faq.q: Can reversed water lines damage clothes?
faq.a: Yes. Hot water on delicate fabrics can cause shrinkage, color bleeding, and fiber damage. Correct the connections as soon as possible.

faq.q: Is 4E1 the same as 4C2?
faq.a: Yes. 4C2 is the updated code on newer Samsung models. Both indicate the hot and cold water connections are reversed.

title: Water Temperature Too Hot During Delicate Cycle
shortDescription: The incoming water temperature exceeds the maximum allowed for the selected delicate or wool cycle.
fullDescription: Error code 4E2 appears when the washer detects that the incoming water temperature is too high for a temperature-sensitive cycle such as Delicates or Wool. Samsung washers protect fabrics by monitoring inlet water temperature and stopping the cycle if it exceeds safe limits. This can occur if the hot water heater is set too high, if water lines are reversed, or during initial use after installation.

causes.cause: Hot water heater temperature set too high (above 130F)
Hot and cold water supply lines reversed
Defective mixing valve not blending cold water properly

symptoms: Error 4E2 on display during delicate cycle
Washer stops at beginning of fill
Water feels scalding hot to touch

fixSteps.title: Lower hot water heater temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Set your home water heater to 120F (49C) or lower. Wait 30 minutes for the temperature to adjust, then retry the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Verify hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure hot and cold water hoses are connected to the correct inlets. See 4E1 fix steps for details.

fixSteps.title: Run cold water before starting
fixSteps.instruction: Run the cold water faucet for 30 seconds before starting a delicate cycle. This clears hot water that may be sitting in the pipes.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists with confirmed correct water temperature, test the inlet thermistor with a multimeter. It should read approximately 40 kOhms at room temperature.

whenToCallPro: This error is usually resolved by adjusting water supply temperature. Call a professional only if the thermistor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 4E2 error code fix

faq.q: Why does my Samsung washer say 4E2 only on delicate?
faq.a: The 4E2 error only triggers on temperature-sensitive cycles. The washer monitors water temperature more strictly on Delicates and Wool to protect fabrics.

faq.q: What temperature should my water heater be set to?
faq.a: For most household use, 120F (49C) is recommended. This prevents scalding and allows your washer to function properly on all cycles.

faq.q: Can I bypass the 4E2 error?
faq.a: No, and you shouldn't. This protection prevents fabric damage from excessively hot water. Lower your water heater temperature instead.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer is unable to drain water from the tub within the allotted time.
fullDescription: Error code 5E is one of the most common Samsung washer errors, indicating the washer cannot pump water out of the tub within approximately 15 minutes. Water remains in the drum after the wash or rinse cycle. This is usually caused by a clogged drain filter, kinked drain hose, or a failed drain pump. It's critical to address this error promptly to prevent water damage and mold growth.

causes.cause: Clogged drain pump filter with debris, coins, or lint
Drain hose inserted too far into standpipe

symptoms: Error code 5E, E2, or 5C on display
Water remains in drum after cycle
Water leaking from drain filter area

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the access panel at the bottom front of the washer. Place towels on the floor and a shallow container under the filter. Slowly turn the filter cap counterclockwise and pull it out. Remove all debris including coins, lint, hair, and small items. Clean the filter under running water and reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer away from the wall. Check the drain hose for kinks or sharp bends. The drain hose should not be inserted more than 6 inches into the standpipe. Ensure the hose has a gentle downward slope to the drain.

fixSteps.title: Clear the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose from the standpipe. Hold it over a bucket to drain remaining water. Check for blockages by running water through the hose. Use a drain snake for stubborn clogs.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump (behind the filter). Listen for the pump running during a drain cycle. If silent, test the pump motor with a multimeter for continuity. The pump motor should read 5-15 ohms. Replace if open or shorted.

fixSteps.title: Check for impeller damage
fixSteps.instruction: With the pump filter removed, look inside the pump housing with a flashlight. Check that the impeller (fan-like blade) spins freely and is not cracked or broken. Replace the pump if the impeller is damaged.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the drain pump motor has failed or if you cannot locate the blockage. Also call a plumber if the household drain itself is clogged.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 5E error code fix

faq.q: How do I drain my Samsung washer manually?
faq.a: Open the small access panel at the bottom front. Place towels down, pull out the small emergency drain hose, and release the cap into a shallow container. The water will drain by gravity.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Samsung recommends cleaning the drain filter monthly or after every 40 wash loads. Regular cleaning prevents 5E errors and pump damage.

faq.q: What is the most common cause of Samsung washer 5E?
faq.a: A clogged drain pump filter is by far the most common cause. Coins, hair ties, lint, and small items accumulate in the filter and restrict water flow.

title: Motor Overcurrent Error
shortDescription: The drive motor is drawing excessive electrical current, triggering overcurrent protection.
fullDescription: Error code 8E (or 8E1 on some models) indicates the motor's current draw has exceeded safe limits. The control board's overcurrent protection circuit has tripped to prevent motor damage. This can be caused by a shorted motor winding, a faulty inverter board, a mechanical obstruction in the drum, or an overloaded washer. On newer models, this may display as 8C or 8C1.

causes.cause: Shorted motor winding drawing excessive current
Faulty inverter or motor control board
Worn drum bearings creating excessive friction

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer and remove excess laundry. Restart with a smaller, balanced load.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to allow the motor and control board to cool and reset. Plug back in and try a short cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for drum obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the empty drum by hand. It should rotate smoothly. Listen for scraping or grinding. Check between the door gasket and drum for trapped items.

fixSteps.title: Test motor current draw
fixSteps.instruction: Using a clamp meter, measure the motor's current draw during operation. Compare to the rating plate on the motor. If current exceeds the rated amperage, the motor or inverter is faulty.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the error persists with an empty drum, as the motor or inverter board likely needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 8E error code fix

faq.q: Can an overloaded washer cause an 8E error?
faq.a: Yes, overloading is a common cause. The motor draws excessive current trying to spin a heavy load. Always follow the manufacturer's load capacity guidelines.

faq.q: Is 8E the same as 8C?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung transitioned from letter+number codes (8E) to number+letter codes (8C) on newer models. They indicate the same motor overcurrent condition.

faq.q: Is it safe to run the washer with an 8E error?
faq.a: No. Running a washer with a motor overcurrent condition can cause permanent motor damage, wiring damage, or even a fire. Resolve the error before use.

title: Low Voltage Error
shortDescription: The washer's power supply voltage is below the minimum operating threshold.
fullDescription: Error code 9E1 indicates the electrical supply voltage to the washer has dropped below the minimum required level (typically below 104V for US 120V models). Low voltage can prevent the motor and heater from operating correctly. This is usually a household electrical issue rather than a washer component failure.

causes.cause: Low household voltage from utility company
Overloaded electrical circuit with too many appliances
Damaged or undersized power cord extension
Loose electrical connections at the outlet
Faulty main control board voltage sensing circuit

symptoms: Other appliances on same circuit also affected

fixSteps.title: Check the power outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter set to AC voltage, measure the voltage at the outlet. It should read between 110V and 125V. Below 104V will trigger this error.

fixSteps.title: Eliminate extension cords
fixSteps.instruction: Plug the washer directly into the wall outlet. Extension cords cause voltage drop, especially with high-draw appliances. Samsung requires a dedicated 20-amp circuit.

fixSteps.title: Reduce circuit load
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug other appliances from the same circuit. Running a dryer, space heater, or other high-draw devices on the same circuit can cause voltage drops.

fixSteps.title: Check outlet connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker. Remove the outlet cover and check that all wire connections are tight and not corroded. Loose connections cause voltage drops under load.

whenToCallPro: Call an electrician if the outlet voltage is consistently low, if the wiring is old or damaged, or if you need a dedicated circuit installed for the washer.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 9E1 error code fix

faq.q: What voltage does a Samsung washer need?
faq.a: Samsung washers in the US require 120V AC, 60Hz on a dedicated 20-amp circuit. The acceptable voltage range is typically 104V-132V.

faq.q: Can a power strip cause 9E1?
faq.a: Yes. Power strips and extension cords add resistance that drops voltage under load. Always plug the washer directly into a properly wired wall outlet.

faq.q: Why does my washer show 9E1 only sometimes?
faq.a: Intermittent 9E1 errors often occur during peak household electrical usage or when the utility company's voltage fluctuates. Monitor your outlet voltage at different times.

title: High Voltage Error
shortDescription: The washer's power supply voltage exceeds the maximum safe operating threshold.
fullDescription: Error code 9E2 indicates the electrical supply voltage is too high for safe washer operation (typically above 132V for US 120V models). Overvoltage can damage sensitive electronic components including the main control board and inverter. This usually indicates a household wiring problem or utility voltage issue.

causes.cause: Faulty household wiring or neutral connection
Voltage spike or surge from nearby equipment
Defective main control board voltage regulator

symptoms: Washer shuts off as protection measure
Control panel lights may be extra bright

fixSteps.title: Measure outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to check the outlet voltage. It should be between 110V and 125V. Readings above 132V indicate an overvoltage condition.

fixSteps.title: Install a surge protector
fixSteps.instruction: Use a heavy-duty surge protector rated for appliances (not a basic power strip). This can protect the washer from voltage spikes.

fixSteps.title: Check other outlets
fixSteps.instruction: Measure voltage at other outlets in your home. If all are high, contact your utility company to check the transformer serving your home.

fixSteps.title: Verify neutral wire connection
fixSteps.instruction: A loose or broken neutral wire can cause overvoltage on 120V circuits. This requires an electrician to inspect the main panel and wiring.

whenToCallPro: Call an electrician immediately if outlet voltage consistently exceeds 130V. Overvoltage is a fire hazard and can damage all electronics in your home.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 9E2 error code fix

faq.q: Is overvoltage dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Sustained overvoltage can damage the washer's electronic control boards and is a fire hazard. Address the cause promptly.

faq.q: Can my utility company cause 9E2?

fixSteps.instruction: Test the heater element for continuity and ground fault. It should show 10-30 ohms between terminals and infinite resistance to ground. A grounded heater can stay on continuously.

shortDescription: The air duct temperature sensor in washer-dryer combo units is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error code tE2 specifically indicates a failure of the duct temperature sensor found in Samsung washer-dryer combination units. This sensor monitors the temperature of air flowing through the drying duct. If the control board cannot get a valid reading, drying operation is disabled to prevent overheating. This error only appears on models with drying functionality.

causes.cause: Damaged sensor wiring from heat exposure

symptoms: Error code tE2 or tC2 on display
Error appears when drying is selected

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart. Attempt a drying cycle to see if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Clean the duct system
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the lint filter and duct pathway. Lint buildup on the sensor can insulate it and cause incorrect readings.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the duct temperature sensor and verify the wire connector is secure. The sensor is usually located in the drying air duct pathway.

fixSteps.title: Test duct temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test resistance with a multimeter. It should read approximately 10 kOhms at room temperature. Replace if open or shorted.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests normal but the error persists, the control board's sensor circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer tE2 error code fix

faq.q: Does tE2 affect the washing function?
faq.a: No. tE2 only affects the drying function. The washing cycles should still operate normally.

faq.q: Is tE2 only for combo units?
faq.a: Yes. The duct temperature sensor is specific to washer-dryer combination units. Standard washers do not have this sensor.

faq.q: Can I dry my clothes in a separate dryer?
faq.a: Yes. If only the drying function is affected, you can use the washer for washing and dry your clothes in a separate dryer.

title: Drain Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The drain water temperature sensor is reporting invalid readings.
fullDescription: Error code tE3 indicates a problem with the temperature sensor that monitors the drain water temperature. This sensor helps the washer optimize rinse cycles and detect potential issues with the heater. Found primarily in higher-end Samsung models, this sensor is located in or near the drain path and can become fouled with lint and debris.

causes.cause: Sensor covered in lint and detergent residue

symptoms: Error code tE3 or tC3 on display
Error during drain or rinse cycles
Washer may complete wash but error on drain
Water temperature not optimized for rinse

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. Run a complete cycle to check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter area
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the drain pump filter and inspect the area around it. The drain temperature sensor may be in this vicinity. Remove any lint, debris, or detergent buildup.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find the drain temperature sensor near the drain path. Check that its connector is secure and the sensor surface is clean.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test with a multimeter. Expected resistance at room temperature is approximately 40 kOhms. Replace if readings are out of range.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test fine but the error persists, have a technician check the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer tE3 error code fix

faq.q: Is tE3 a common error?
faq.a: No. tE3 is relatively uncommon and only appears on models equipped with a drain water temperature sensor, typically higher-end front-loaders.

faq.q: Can I ignore tE3?
faq.a: The washer may still operate with this error, but rinse cycle optimization and temperature monitoring will be compromised. Repair is recommended.

faq.q: Where is the drain temperature sensor?
faq.a: It's located in or near the drain path, often close to the drain pump or the sump at the bottom of the tub.

title: Voltage Fluctuation Error
shortDescription: The washer is experiencing ongoing power supply voltage fluctuations outside acceptable limits.
fullDescription: Error code UC indicates the washer is detecting continuous voltage fluctuations that prevent stable operation. Unlike 9E1 (low voltage) or 9E2 (high voltage) which indicate a consistent under/over voltage, UC indicates the voltage is rapidly changing. This is typically caused by an unstable power grid, a generator, or electrical interference from large equipment cycling on and off.

causes.cause: Generator power with poor voltage regulation
Large appliances cycling on same circuit
Loose neutral connection in home wiring

symptoms: May appear during high-draw phases like spin

fixSteps.title: Check for voltage stability
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to monitor outlet voltage for several minutes. Normal is 110-125V steady. Fluctuations of more than 10V indicate an unstable supply.

fixSteps.title: Use a dedicated circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the washer is on its own dedicated 20-amp circuit. Other appliances on the same circuit can cause voltage drops when they cycle.

fixSteps.title: Install a voltage stabilizer
fixSteps.instruction: A plug-in voltage stabilizer or power conditioner can smooth out fluctuations and provide consistent voltage to the washer.

fixSteps.title: Check electrical panel
fixSteps.instruction: Have an electrician inspect your electrical panel for loose connections, especially the neutral bus bar. Loose neutrals cause voltage fluctuations.

whenToCallPro: Call an electrician if voltage fluctuations are caused by household wiring issues rather than utility problems.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer UC error code fix

faq.q: Is UC a Samsung washer problem or an electrical problem?
faq.a: UC is almost always an electrical supply issue, not a washer malfunction. The washer is correctly detecting unstable voltage.

faq.q: Can I use the washer during a UC error?
faq.a: The washer may pause and resume as voltage fluctuates. It's best to resolve the voltage issue first to prevent potential damage to the control board.

faq.q: Will a UPS help with UC errors?
faq.a: A UPS designed for appliances can help, but a voltage stabilizer or power conditioner is more appropriate for washing machines.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load that prevents safe spinning.
fullDescription: Error code UE is one of the most common Samsung washer errors, indicating the laundry load has shifted to one side of the drum, creating an imbalance. The washer has attempted to redistribute the load by slowly tumbling and adjusting, but cannot achieve sufficient balance for the high-speed spin cycle. Persistent imbalance can damage the drum bearings, suspension springs, and shock absorbers if the washer is forced to spin.

causes.cause: Single heavy item like a towel or blanket
Too few items in the drum
Worn suspension springs or shock absorbers
Washer not level on the floor

symptoms: Error code UE, E4, DC, or Ub on display
Drum slowly tumbles trying to balance

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and redistribute the laundry evenly around the drum. Mix heavy and light items. Ensure clothing is not balled up or tangled together.

fixSteps.title: Add or remove items
fixSteps.instruction: If washing a single heavy item (like a bath mat), add a few towels for balance. If the drum is too full, remove some items. The drum should be loosely filled 3/4 full.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Place a bubble level on top of the washer. Adjust the leveling feet by turning them clockwise (lower) or counterclockwise (raise). All four feet must be firmly on the floor. Lock the lock nuts against the washer base.

fixSteps.title: Check shock absorbers and springs
fixSteps.instruction: If UE occurs frequently with normal loads, inspect the shock absorbers (bottom front) and suspension springs (top). Worn shocks allow excessive drum movement. Replace if leaking fluid or not providing resistance.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the washer frequently shows UE even with balanced loads, as the suspension system (shocks and springs) may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer UE error code fix

faq.q: How do I prevent UE errors?
faq.a: Wash similar items together, don't overload or underload, mix heavy and light items, and ensure the washer is properly leveled.

faq.q: Can one towel cause a UE error?
faq.a: Yes. A single heavy wet item creates an extreme imbalance. Always wash at least 2-3 similar items for proper balance.

faq.q: Is it normal to get UE occasionally?
faq.a: Occasional UE errors with unusual loads are normal. Frequent UE errors with regular loads suggest a leveling, shock absorber, or spring issue.

title: Excessive Suds Detected
shortDescription: The washer has detected too many suds in the drum, which can impair washing and draining.
fullDescription: The Sud (or 5ud) error indicates the washer has detected excessive suds. Samsung washers use suds sensors to monitor foam levels. Too many suds reduce washing effectiveness, interfere with the water level sensor, prevent proper draining, and can cause leaks. The washer will pause the cycle and attempt to reduce suds with extra rinse cycles. This error is almost always caused by using too much detergent or using non-HE detergent in a front-load washer.

causes.cause: Using non-HE (regular) detergent in a front-loader

symptoms: Error code Sud or 5ud on display
Extra rinse cycles being added automatically
Drain cycle takes longer than normal

fixSteps.title: Wait for automatic suds reduction
fixSteps.instruction: The washer will attempt to dissolve suds automatically. Wait 5-10 minutes. The washer may add extra rinses. Do not open the door.

fixSteps.title: Run extra rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: When the error clears temporarily, select an extra Rinse+Spin cycle to flush remaining suds from the system.

fixSteps.title: Reduce detergent amount
fixSteps.instruction: For future loads, use only HE detergent and measure carefully. For most Samsung washers, use 2 tablespoons or less. If you have soft water, use even less.

fixSteps.title: Clean the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Run an empty cycle on the hottest setting with no detergent to flush residual suds from the drain system, pump, and hoses. Samsung washers have a Self Clean or Pure Cycle mode for this purpose.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Excessive suds can leave residue in the drain pump filter. Clean the filter to prevent future drain issues.

whenToCallPro: This error rarely requires professional service. If suds errors persist even with correct detergent use, the suds sensor or drain system may need inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer Sud error code fix

faq.q: What detergent should I use in my Samsung front-load washer?
faq.a: Always use HE (High Efficiency) detergent. Regular detergent creates too many suds in front-loaders. Look for the HE symbol on the detergent packaging.

faq.q: How much detergent should I use?
faq.a: Use 2 tablespoons or less of HE liquid detergent for a normal load. With soft water, use even less. More detergent does not mean cleaner clothes.

faq.q: Is the Sud error harmful to my washer?
faq.a: Occasional suds errors are not harmful. However, chronic over-sudsing can damage the water level sensor, cause leaks, and leave residue in the drain system.

title: No Fill / Water Not Entering
shortDescription: The washer is not detecting any water flow during the fill phase.
fullDescription: Error code nF (or nF1) means no water has entered the tub during the fill cycle. While similar to 4E (water supply error), nF specifically indicates zero water detected rather than insufficient flow. This usually means the water supply is completely shut off, both inlet valve solenoids have failed, or there is a complete blockage in the supply system.

causes.cause: Complete blockage in both inlet valve screens

symptoms: Error code nF or nF1 on display
No water entering the drum at all
Washer runs fill timer but drum remains empty
Error appears at very beginning of cycle

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Check that both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer are fully open. Try running water from another nearby faucet to verify water service is active.

fixSteps.title: Check for frozen hoses
fixSteps.instruction: In cold weather, water supply hoses running through unheated areas can freeze. Feel the hoses — frozen hoses will be rigid and cold. Thaw with warm towels (never use an open flame).

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water. Disconnect both hoses from the washer. Remove and clean the mesh filter screens. Check for complete blockage by sediment or mineral deposits.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve solenoids
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test each inlet valve solenoid for continuity. Each should read 500-1500 ohms. If both are open (no continuity), the entire valve assembly needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If water supply is confirmed available but no water enters the washer, the inlet valve assembly has likely failed and may need professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer nF error code fix

faq.q: What is the difference between nF and 4E?
faq.a: nF means absolutely no water was detected entering the tub. 4E means insufficient water flow. nF usually indicates a completely closed supply or failed valve.

faq.q: Can frozen pipes cause nF?
faq.a: Yes. If supply hoses or household pipes freeze, no water can reach the washer. Thaw gently with warm towels and insulate exposed pipes.

faq.q: Should I call a plumber or appliance repair?
faq.a: If the problem is the household water supply, call a plumber. If the washer's inlet valve has failed, call an appliance repair technician.

title: Door Open During Cycle
shortDescription: The door was opened or detected as unlatched during an active wash cycle.
fullDescription: Error code DC (or DC1) indicates the washer door opened or the door lock disengaged during a running cycle. This is a safety error that immediately stops the drum. On top-load models, this triggers when the lid is opened during the spin cycle. Unlike dE (door won't lock at start), DC occurs when the lock releases mid-cycle, which may indicate a failing door lock mechanism.

causes.cause: Vibration causing door latch to disengage

symptoms: Error code DC or DC1 on display

fixSteps.title: Close and restart
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the door is fully closed. Press the latch firmly. Press Start/Pause to resume the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for interference
fixSteps.instruction: Make sure nothing is preventing the door from staying closed — children, pets, items leaning against the washer. Check that the washer is not vibrating excessively.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: An unlevel washer vibrates excessively during spin, which can jar the door latch loose. Level the washer using adjustable feet.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the door lock is releasing on its own, the lock mechanism is failing. Replace the door lock assembly. See dE fix steps for replacement procedure.

whenToCallPro: If the door lock releases during every spin cycle, the lock assembly needs replacement. This is a safety issue that should be addressed promptly.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer DC error code fix

faq.q: Is it safe if the door opens during spin?
faq.a: No. The drum can be spinning at 1000+ RPM. The door lock is a critical safety device. If it releases during spin, stop using the washer until repaired.

faq.q: Can vibration cause DC error?
faq.a: Yes. Excessive vibration from an unlevel washer or unbalanced load can jar the door latch loose, triggering a DC error.

faq.q: Is DC the same as dE?
faq.a: They're related but different. dE means the door won't lock at the start. DC means the door opened or unlocked during an active cycle.

title: Add Wash Door Error
shortDescription: The Add Wash (or AddWash) door on compatible Samsung washers is not properly closed.
fullDescription: Error code DC3 specifically applies to Samsung washers equipped with the AddWash mini door feature. This small secondary door on the main door allows you to add forgotten items mid-cycle. DC3 indicates the AddWash door is not fully closed or its latch sensor is not detecting closure. The washer will not operate with the AddWash door open.

causes.cause: AddWash door not fully clicked shut

symptoms: Error after adding items through AddWash door
AddWash door appears closed but error persists

fixSteps.title: Close the AddWash door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Push the AddWash mini door firmly until you hear it click. It requires a positive click to register as closed.

fixSteps.title: Clean the AddWash latch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the AddWash door. Clean the latch mechanism and the mating surface on the main door with a damp cloth. Remove any lint or detergent residue.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the AddWash door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the AddWash door gasket for damage or deformation. A warped gasket can prevent the door from closing properly.

fixSteps.title: Test AddWash door sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the door closes firmly but the error persists, the door sensor/switch may be faulty. Access requires removing the main door assembly inner panel to reach the AddWash latch mechanism.

whenToCallPro: If the AddWash door sensor has failed, replacement requires partial disassembly of the main door and is best done by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer DC3 error code fix

faq.q: What Samsung washers have the AddWash door?
faq.a: The AddWash feature is available on select Samsung front-load models, typically mid-range and premium models from 2016 onwards.

faq.q: Can I use the washer without the AddWash feature?
faq.a: Yes, but the AddWash door must still be fully closed. You don't have to use the feature, but the door sensor must detect it's shut.

faq.q: How does the AddWash door work?
faq.a: After starting a cycle, press Pause, then open the AddWash mini door to add forgotten items. Close the mini door, then press Start to resume.

title: Drain Error (Legacy Code)
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water within the allotted time (legacy display format).
fullDescription: Error code E2 is the legacy version of the 5E/5C drain error code used on older Samsung washer models. It indicates the exact same condition: the washer cannot pump water out of the tub within approximately 15 minutes. On newer Samsung washers, this error displays as 5E or 5C. All troubleshooting steps for 5E apply to E2.

causes.cause: Drain hose positioned too far into standpipe

symptoms: Washer won't advance to spin cycle

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the access panel at the bottom front. Place towels down. Slowly open the filter cap and remove all debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks. Ensure it's not pushed more than 6 inches into the standpipe.

fixSteps.title: Clear drain hose blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect drain hose and flush with water. Use a drain snake for stubborn clogs.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for pump running during drain cycle. Test motor with multimeter if silent.

whenToCallPro: See 5E error code for comprehensive troubleshooting. Call a professional if the drain pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer E2 error code fix

faq.q: Is E2 the same as 5E?
faq.a: Yes. E2 is the older code format. Newer Samsung washers display this as 5E or 5C. The troubleshooting is identical.

faq.q: Why did Samsung change from E2 to 5E?
faq.a: Samsung updated their error code system to use alphanumeric codes that more clearly relate to the affected system (5=drain).

faq.q: How do I manually drain the washer with E2?
faq.a: Use the emergency drain hose behind the small access panel at the bottom front of the washer. Pull out the small hose and release the cap.

title: Overflow Error (Legacy Code)
shortDescription: The water level in the tub has exceeded the maximum safe level (legacy display format).
fullDescription: Error code E3 is the legacy version of the OE/OC overflow error code used on older Samsung washer models. It indicates water has risen above the overflow sensor. On newer models, this displays as OE or OC. See the OE error for comprehensive troubleshooting.

causes.cause: Main control board not closing valve

symptoms: Water visible through door glass above fill line

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately close both water supply faucets behind the washer to stop water flow.

fixSteps.title: Drain excess water
fixSteps.instruction: Run a Drain/Spin cycle or use the emergency drain hose to remove excess water.

fixSteps.title: Check for drain backup
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect drain hose from standpipe. If water flows back, the household drain is clogged.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn on water and start a fill cycle. If water keeps flowing after the tub fills, the inlet valve is stuck open. Replace the valve.

whenToCallPro: If water is actively overflowing, turn off water supply immediately. See OE error for complete troubleshooting.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer E3 error code fix

faq.q: Is E3 the same as OE?
faq.a: Yes. E3 is the older code format. Newer Samsung washers display this as OE or OC.

faq.q: Is E3 an emergency?
faq.a: It can be. If the washer is actively overflowing, turn off the water supply and unplug the washer immediately.

faq.q: What causes a Samsung washer to overfill?
faq.a: The most common cause is a water inlet valve that's stuck in the open position, allowing water to flow continuously.

title: Water Supply Error (New Code Format)
shortDescription: The washer is not detecting adequate water supply during the fill cycle.
fullDescription: Error code 4C is the modern equivalent of the 4E error code on newer Samsung washing machines. It indicates the washer cannot fill with water within the expected timeframe. All causes and fixes are identical to the 4E error. Samsung transitioned to this numbering format on models manufactured from approximately 2017 onwards.
symptoms: No fill sound when cycle begins

fixSteps.title: Check water faucets
fixSteps.instruction: Verify both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect supply hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Check for kinks, bends, or damage in both supply hoses.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filters
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, disconnect hoses from washer, remove and clean the mesh filter screens inside each inlet.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Test inlet valve solenoids with multimeter. Each should read 500-1500 ohms.

whenToCallPro: See 4E error code for complete troubleshooting details.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 4C error code fix

faq.q: Is 4C the same as 4E?
faq.a: Yes. 4C is the updated code on newer Samsung models. Both indicate a water supply problem.

faq.q: Why do some Samsung washers show 4C and others show 4E?
faq.a: Samsung updated their error code format around 2017. Older models show 4E, newer models show 4C. Same issue, different display.

faq.q: What should I check first for 4C?
faq.a: Check that both water faucets behind the washer are fully turned on. This is the most common and easiest fix.

title: Hot/Cold Water Lines Reversed (New Code Format)
shortDescription: The hot and cold water supply connections are swapped.
fullDescription: Error code 4C2 is the modern equivalent of the 4E1 error code. It means the washer detects hot water on the cold inlet and cold water on the hot inlet. See 4E1 for complete troubleshooting.

causes.cause: Water hoses connected to wrong inlets
Wall faucets connected to wrong supply lines

fixSteps.title: Check hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Verify hot hose connects to hot inlet (H/red) and cold hose to cold inlet (C/blue) at back of washer.

fixSteps.title: Swap hoses if reversed
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off faucets, disconnect both hoses, reconnect correctly.

fixSteps.title: Test faucet output
fixSteps.instruction: Run water from each faucet to verify hot produces hot and cold produces cold.

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: Plug in and run a warm cycle to verify correct temperatures.

whenToCallPro: See 4E1 for complete details. This is almost always resolved by swapping the supply hoses.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 4C2 error code fix

faq.q: Is 4C2 the same as 4E1?
faq.a: Yes. 4C2 is the updated display format. Both indicate reversed hot and cold water connections.

faq.q: How can I tell which inlet is hot and cold?
faq.a: Look at the rear of the washer. Hot is typically marked H with a red indicator. Cold is marked C with a blue indicator.

faq.q: Can 4C2 damage my clothes?
faq.a: Yes. Unexpected hot water can shrink, fade, or damage delicate fabrics. Correct the connections promptly.

title: Drain Error (New Code Format)
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water from the tub.
fullDescription: Error code 5C is the modern equivalent of the 5E error code on newer Samsung models. The washer cannot pump out water within 15 minutes. See 5E for complete troubleshooting. This is one of the most common washer errors across all Samsung models.
symptoms: Drain pump sounds but water remains

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open bottom access panel, place towels down, remove filter cap, clear all debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks. Don't insert more than 6 inches into standpipe.

fixSteps.title: Clear drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and flush the drain hose. Snake if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for pump operation. Test motor with multimeter (5-15 ohms expected).

whenToCallPro: See 5E error for comprehensive troubleshooting.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 5C error code fix

faq.q: Is 5C the same as 5E?
faq.a: Yes. 5C is the updated code format on newer Samsung washers.

faq.q: How do I manually drain a Samsung washer?
faq.a: Use the emergency drain hose behind the small access door at the bottom front. Pull the hose out and uncap it over a shallow container.

faq.q: Should I clean my drain filter regularly?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung recommends monthly cleaning to prevent 5C/5E drain errors.

title: Motor Current Error (New Code Format)
shortDescription: The motor is drawing abnormal current levels.
fullDescription: Error code 8C is the modern equivalent of the 8E error code. It indicates the drive motor current is outside acceptable parameters. 8C1 indicates overcurrent and 8C2 indicates specific current waveform abnormalities. See 8E for complete troubleshooting.

fixSteps.title: Reduce load
fixSteps.instruction: Remove excess laundry and restart with a smaller load.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes to allow motor cooling and board reset.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Spin empty drum by hand. Should rotate smoothly without grinding.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor windings with multimeter (2-7 ohms between each pair of terminals).

whenToCallPro: See 8E for complete details. Professional service recommended for motor or inverter replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 8C error code fix

faq.q: Is 8C the same as 8E?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung updated the code format. 8C = 8E, 8C1 = 8E1.

faq.q: Can I still use the washer with 8C?
faq.a: No. Motor overcurrent is a safety concern. Do not operate the washer until the issue is resolved.

faq.q: Is 8C usually expensive to fix?
faq.a: If it's just an overloaded drum, it's free. Motor or inverter replacement can cost $200-450 with labor.

title: Low Voltage Error (New Code Format)
shortDescription: Power supply voltage is below minimum operating threshold.

fixSteps.title: Remove extension cords
fixSteps.instruction: Plug washer directly into wall outlet.

fixSteps.title: Reduce circuit load
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug other appliances from same circuit.

fixSteps.title: Check outlet wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Have electrician check for loose connections if voltage is consistently low.

whenToCallPro: See 9E1 for complete troubleshooting. Call an electrician for persistent low voltage.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 9C1 error code fix

faq.q: Is 9C1 the same as 9E1?
faq.a: Yes. 9C1 is the newer code format for the same low voltage condition.

faq.q: What causes low voltage?
faq.a: Common causes include overloaded circuits, extension cords, loose wiring, and utility supply issues.

faq.q: What voltage does my Samsung washer need?
faq.a: Samsung washers require 120V AC (acceptable range 104V-132V) on a dedicated 20-amp circuit.

title: High Voltage Error (New Code Format)
shortDescription: Power supply voltage exceeds maximum safe operating threshold.
fullDescription: Error code 9C2 is the modern equivalent of the 9E2 high voltage error. See 9E2 for complete troubleshooting. Overvoltage can damage the washer's electronic components.

fixSteps.title: Measure voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Check outlet with multimeter. Above 132V indicates overvoltage.

fixSteps.title: Install surge protector
fixSteps.instruction: Use an appliance-grade surge protector.

fixSteps.title: Check other outlets
fixSteps.instruction: Measure voltage throughout home. Contact utility if all are high.

fixSteps.title: Check neutral wire
fixSteps.instruction: Have electrician inspect for loose neutral connection.

whenToCallPro: See 9E2 for details. Call an electrician immediately for persistent overvoltage.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer 9C2 error code fix

faq.q: Is 9C2 the same as 9E2?
faq.a: Yes. Same overvoltage condition, newer code format.

faq.q: Is overvoltage dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. It can damage electronics and is a fire hazard. Address immediately.

faq.q: Who do I call for high voltage?
faq.a: Call your utility company to check the transformer, and an electrician to check your home wiring.

Display shows code but no response to buttons

fixSteps.title: Power cycle

fixSteps.title: Check cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all ribbon cables and wire harnesses between control boards.

fixSteps.title: Clean connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Use electrical contact cleaner on connector pins.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If connections are good, a control board has likely failed.

whenToCallPro: See AE for complete details. Professional diagnosis recommended if connections are intact.

faq.q: Is AC the same as AE?
faq.a: Yes. AC is the updated code. AC6 indicates 6 consecutive communication failures.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause AC?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage communication circuits on control boards.

faq.q: How much does control board replacement cost?
faq.a: Main board: $100-280 for part. Display board: $60-150. Plus $100-200 labor.

title: Clutch Error (Top-Load Models)
shortDescription: The washer's clutch mechanism is not engaging or disengaging properly.
fullDescription: Error code PC (or PC1) is specific to Samsung top-load washers with a clutch mechanism. The clutch transitions the washer between agitation mode and spin mode. When the clutch fails to engage or disengage properly, the washer cannot shift between wash and spin operations. This error may also appear if the clutch motor or position sensor fails.

causes.cause: Main control board clutch control circuit failure

symptoms: Error code PC or PC1 on display
Washer won't shift from wash to spin
Clicking sounds from bottom of washer

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. Restart and run a Spin Only cycle to test clutch engagement.

fixSteps.title: Listen for clutch motor
fixSteps.instruction: During the transition from wash to spin, listen for the clutch motor actuating (a brief mechanical sound from below). No sound indicates a clutch motor issue.

fixSteps.title: Check clutch motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the clutch motor beneath the washer tub. Verify the wire connector is firmly attached.

fixSteps.title: Test clutch motor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the clutch motor with a multimeter. Replace if open circuit. The clutch motor actuates a cam that shifts the drive mechanism.

whenToCallPro: Clutch replacement often requires removing the tub and inner basket. Professional service is recommended for full clutch replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung washer PC error code fix

faq.q: Do front-load Samsung washers have clutches?
faq.a: No. PC errors only apply to top-load washers. Front-loaders use direct-drive motors without clutch mechanisms.

faq.q: What does the clutch do in a top-load washer?
faq.a: The clutch shifts the transmission between agitation mode (back and forth) and spin mode (continuous rotation). It's similar to a transmission in a car.

faq.q: Can I replace the clutch myself?
faq.a: It's a difficult DIY repair as it requires removing the inner tub basket and accessing components deep in the machine. Most homeowners prefer professional service.
